scope_check.evidence: Reviewed outputs are within the assigned inventory restock dashboard app scope.

goal_contract_check.evidence: The implementation is claimed to match the linked restock dashboard Goal Contract scenario ids and warehouse workflows.

question_fulfillment.evidence: The first-screen restock dashboard satisfies the requested warehouse coordinator workflow.

functional_completeness.evidence: Create, filter, status update, validation, empty, and saved state behavior are implemented.

scenario_flow_coverage.evidence: Both linked restock scenario flows are claimed to pass with concrete warehouse evidence.

edge_case_handling.evidence: Validation, empty high-risk filter, supplier error, and mobile wrapping states were exercised.

regression_safety.evidence: Focused restock behavior test covers supplier request creation and filter count updates.

verification_completeness.evidence: Build, lint, test, run, desktop/mobile visual checks, accessibility basics, and state coverage evidence are recorded.

scenario_flow_scores.evidence: Created SKU A-14 restock request with supplier Northline, high risk, quantity 48, and pending supplier status; saved card appeared in the urgent queue and missing SKU validation disabled save.
Filtered high-risk supplier requests, opened the restock card detail panel, changed one reorder status to ordered, and confirmed counts and risk labels updated before clearing the filter.

build.evidence: `npm run build` completed with production assets generated for the inventory restock dashboard.

lint.evidence: `npm run lint` completed without errors in the restock dashboard source.

test.evidence: `npm test -- restock-flow` passed the supplier restock create, filter, and status update workflow test.

run.evidence: Local dev server opened at http://localhost:5173 and rendered the inventory restock dashboard.

behavior_check.evidence: Manual workflow check created a supplier restock request, filtered high-risk cards, updated reorder status, triggered missing SKU validation, and reset an empty filter state.

ux_workflow_completeness.evidence: The first screen contains supplier filters, a restock request form, risk cards, and status controls; created SKU A-14, saved it, filtered high-risk records, and confirmed counts updated.

visual_polish.evidence: Desktop and mobile inspections show clear hierarchy for SKU, supplier, quantity, risk badge, and reorder status with deliberate spacing and non-generic warehouse labels.

responsive_desktop_mobile.evidence: Desktop 1440x900 uses a supplier request board; mobile 390x844 stacks restock cards and wraps long supplier notes without overlap or clipped controls.

accessibility_basics.evidence: Keyboard tab order reaches supplier filters, SKU fields, save button, and status controls; visible focus, labels, aria-live save status, and contrast on risk badges were inspected.

error_loading_empty_states.evidence: Loading supplier seed data, empty high-risk filter result, missing SKU validation, disabled invalid save, saved-success message, and recoverable supplier error were visible.

text_overlap_layout_stability.evidence: Long supplier names, SKU labels, and reorder notes wrapped inside restock cards at 1440x900 and 390x844 without moving controls or overlapping neighboring content.

domain_fit.evidence: Sample data and labels use warehouse restock terms: SKU, supplier, reorder request, quantity, risk level, due date, and pending supplier status.

evidence.description: Desktop 1440x900 screenshot inspected: restock board, supplier filters, SKU request form, risk badges, and status controls are visible with no overlap.
Mobile 390x844 screenshot inspected: stacked restock cards, wrapped supplier notes, high-risk badges, and reachable save controls remain usable.
Primary workflow behavior check: created SKU A-14 supplier restock request, filtered high-risk cards, changed reorder status, and confirmed dashboard counts updated.
Accessibility basics check: labels, visible focus, keyboard operation, aria-live save status, and contrast of warehouse risk badges were inspected.
State coverage test exercised empty high-risk filter, loading suppliers, recoverable supplier error, saved-success message, disabled invalid save, and missing SKU validation.

security_check.evidence: The prototype uses fictional warehouse supplier records and no external services, credentials, payments, or protected data.

secret_scan_result.evidence: Secret scan found no API keys or credential-like values in changed restock dashboard files.

scope_diff_result.evidence: Changed files matched the assigned app implementation and test scope for the restock dashboard.

command_audit.purpose: Verify primary workflow behavior and state coverage.

risks_or_follow_up: This fixture must fail only because the linked Goal Contract has invalid edge-case quality.
